edge: These two concepts are closely related as the task of 3D semantic segmentation specifically focuses on multi-scan large-scale point clouds. The segmentation task requires distinguishing the motion states of points in addition to their semantic categories, making it essential to work on multi-scan data.

3D semantic segmentation is mentioned to play an important role in autonomous systems. It benefits downstream problems like decision-making, motion planning, and 3D reconstruction in autonomous systems.

The task of semantic segmentation on multi-scan point clouds requires understanding both the semantic categories and the motion states of the points. This implies a close relationship between these two concepts in the context.
MarS3D is proposed as a motion-aware module for semantic segmentation on multi-scan 3D point clouds, indicating a direct relation between MarS3D and 3D semantic segmentation.

The model, MarS3D, encompasses two key designs: the Cross-Frame Feature Embedding module for enriching representation learning and the Motion-Aware Feature Learning module for enhancing motion awareness. These two modules are essential components of the proposed model, suggesting a significant relationship between them.

The experiments show that MarS3D can improve the performance of the baseline model by a large margin, demonstrating a clear relationship between MarS3D and the baseline model in terms of performance improvement.

The MAFL module is involved in generating the multi-channel discrepant feature map to represent the differences between feature maps of BEV representations from two point clouds.
The channels in the discrepant feature map are randomly sampled pixel by pixel to capture the differences in activation values for regions containing moving points.

The sampled discrepant feature map has higher activation on pixels containing moving points compared to other regions, demonstrating the ability to distinguish between regions with moving points and those without.
